diskStorageTimeouts.description: Repeated storage timeout events may indicate disk, controller, path, or driver risk. This is an early warning signal only, not an exact failure prediction.
diskStorageTimeouts.recommendation: Review System event log details, confirm backups are current, inspect storage controller and disk health, and schedule maintenance if the pattern continues.

diskRetryEvents.description: Repeated disk retry warnings can indicate increasing storage instability. Treat this as a risk indicator rather than a remaining-life estimate.
diskRetryEvents.recommendation: Check disk, cable, controller, and firmware status; compare with physical disk health signals where available.

fileSystemCorruption.description: File system corruption events may indicate storage or OS consistency risk and should be investigated promptly.
fileSystemCorruption.recommendation: Verify backups, review disk and volume health, and plan a maintenance window for deeper file system checks if needed.

networkLinkIssues.description: Repeated link state changes or low link speed can indicate NIC, cable, switch port, driver, or configuration risk.
networkLinkIssues.recommendation: Check adapter status, negotiated speed, cabling, switch port errors, and recent driver or firmware changes.

hardwareSensorWarnings.description: Hardware sensor warnings from management interfaces may indicate component risk. Optional hardware integration is disabled by default in sample configs.
hardwareSensorWarnings.recommendation: Review the vendor management console for power, fan, thermal, memory, and storage controller alerts before planning maintenance.
